judgments.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e == 0`.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E-driven BRAF dependence is a canonical MAPK oncogene-addiction relationship in melanoma and other cancers.

judgments.text: Cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 0`.
judgments.rationale: EGFR amplification as a predictor of EGFR pathway dependence is an established oncology and functional-genomics association.

judgments.text: The apparent BRAF dependency is enriched in `lineage == "skin"` cell lines, which should have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than other lineages.
judgments.rationale: Enrichment of BRAF dependency in skin-lineage melanoma models reflects the well-known prevalence and functional importance of BRAF V600E in melanoma.

judgments.text: Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 0`.
judgments.rationale: ERBB2/HER2 amplification causing ERBB2 dependence is a standard oncogene-addiction paradigm across HER2-amplified cancers.

judgments.text: Cell lines with `kras_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation == 0`.
judgments.rationale: KRAS-mutant cancer models showing KRAS dependency is a well-established functional-genomics finding.

judgments.text: `lineage == "pancreatic"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than non-pancreatic cell lines.
judgments.rationale: Pancreatic cancer cell lines being KRAS-dependent is a standard consequence of the high prevalence of oncogenic KRAS in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

judgments.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss == 0`.
judgments.rationale: NF1 loss predicting RIT1 dependency is a specific synthetic-lethal or bypass-signaling claim that is not a standard oncology consensus association.

judgments.text: The `dependency_RIT1` effect is stronger in the subgroup with `lineage == "brain"` and `nf1_loss == 1` than in all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: A brain-lineage NF1-loss subgroup with stronger RIT1 dependency is a conditional lineage-specific claim beyond established guideline or textbook consensus.

judgments.text: Cell lines with `lineage == "colorectal"` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
judgments.rationale: Colorectal-lineage enrichment for KIF18A dependency is not a standard disease-lineage dependency paradigm in oncology references.

judgments.text: Cell lines with `apc_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation == 0`.
judgments.rationale: APC mutation predicting KIF18A dependency is a mechanistic cross-pathway hypothesis rather than an established functional-genomics consensus association.

judgments.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `wnt_activity_score` (`wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than the remaining cell lines.
judgments.rationale: A WNT-activity-stratified KIF18A dependency is a specific functional-genomics claim not part of standard oncology or DepMap consensus.

judgments.text: The strongest `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup is the complete subgroup with `lineage == "colorectal"` and `apc_mutation == 1`, which has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: APC-mutant colorectal cancer as the strongest KIF18A-dependent subgroup is a more specific subgroup-conditional claim than the established broad CIN/aneuploidy-KIF18A vulnerability.

judgments.text: A related `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup with `lineage == "colorectal"` and `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: Colorectal high-WNT status predicting KIF18A dependency is a specific lineage and pathway interaction not established as canonical.

judgments.text: High aneuploidy alone (`aneuploidy_score` in the top quartile) makes `dependency_KIF18A` scores more negative than in other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: KIF18A dependency in highly aneuploid or chromosomally unstable cancer cells is an established functional-genomics association.

judgments.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing lines.
judgments.rationale: Fast growth rate predicting TMED10 dependency is not a standard oncology or functional-genomics paradigm.

judgments.text: `lineage == "colorectal"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
judgments.rationale: Colorectal lineage-specific TMED10 dependency is not a recognized consensus dependency association.

judgments.text: The subgroup with `lineage_subtype == "luminal"` and `stk11_loss == 1` has more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: Luminal subtype with STK11 loss predicting TMED10 dependency is a speculative subgroup-specific claim outside established references.

judgments.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669`) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than lower-MYC cell lines.
judgments.rationale: MYC-high or MYC-driven cancer dependency on DHX9 has been reported as a recognized preclinical functional-genomics relationship.

judgments.text: The subgroup with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` has more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: The combined high-growth and high-MYC subgroup for DHX9 dependency is a more conditional claim than the known MYC-DHX9 relationship.

judgments.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `rnaseq_SLFN11_log2_tpm` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than lower-SLFN11 cell lines.
judgments.rationale: SLFN11 expression predicting DHX9 dependency is not an established consensus association, unlike its known links to DNA-damage response therapies.

judgments.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing lines.
judgments.rationale: A growth-rate-stratified POLR2A dependency association is a functional-genomics subgroup claim rather than a standard oncology or DepMap consensus association.

judgments.text: The subgroup with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `copy_number_9p21 <= -0.497` has more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: The combined high-growth and 9p21-loss subgroup predicting stronger POLR2A dependency is a conditional cross-feature claim not established as a canonical oncology dependency paradigm.

judgments.text: Low `copy_number_9p21` alone (`copy_number_9p21 <= -0.497`) makes `dependency_POLR2A` scores more negative than in other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: POLR2A vulnerability from 17p/POLR2A copy-number loss is known, but 9p21 loss alone is not a standard established predictor, making this claim more likely a non-canonical pipeline finding; however as stated it resembles a broad copy-number-loss dependency hypothesis rather than a guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Cell lines with `myc_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `myc_amplification == 0`.
judgments.rationale: MYC-driven ribosome biogenesis and ribosomal/protein-synthesis dependencies, including RPL11-related biology, are established cancer biology concepts.

judgments.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `copy_number_8q24` (`copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428`) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: 8q24 copy-number gain is a common proxy for MYC amplification, so an RPL11 dependency association with high 8q24 largely recapitulates a known MYC-ribosome axis.

judgments.text: The subgroup with `myc_amplification == 1` and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` has more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning MYC amplification-associated RPL11 dependency on high proliferation rate is a more specific subgroup interaction than standard MYC-ribosomal dependency consensus.

judgments.text: A systematic single-feature and pair-feature dependency-heterogeneity search should recover the strongest subgroup for each dependency outcome, including canonical driver dependencies and non-canonical subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Systematic single- and pair-feature searches recovering canonical and non-canonical dependency subgroups is a methodological expectation of CRISPR-screen heterogeneity analyses, not a novel biological hypothesis.

judgments.text: For `dependency_KIF18A`, the best complete subgroup from the systematic search is `lineage == "colorectal"` and `apc_mutation == 1`, with more negative scores than all other cell lines.
judgments.rationale: A colorectal APC-mutant subgroup as the best complete predictor of KIF18A dependency is not a canonical DepMap or oncology-reference dependency association.

judgments.text: For housekeeping-like outcomes `dependency_TMED10`, `dependency_DHX9`, and `dependency_POLR2A`, the best-supported heterogeneity is mainly related to high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, with only weak molecular subgroup effects.
judgments.rationale: The claim that housekeeping-like dependencies are mainly growth-rate-driven with weak molecular effects is an interpretive cross-outcome functional-genomics finding rather than a standard disease-lineage consensus.

judgments.text: The best-supported multi-feature subgroup for `dependency_BRAF` is `braf_v600e=1`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=0.964`, and `pten_loss=1`, with more negative dependency than all other lines.

judgments.text: The best-supported multi-feature subgroup for `dependency_EGFR` is `egfr_amplification=1`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=0.964`, and `copy_number_8q24>=0.428`, with more negative dependency than all other lines.

judgments.text: The best-supported multi-feature subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2` is `erbb2_amplification=1`, `lineage=breast`, and `ifn_gamma_signature>=0.519`, with more negative dependency than all other lines.

judgments.text: The best-supported multi-feature subgroup for `dependency_KRAS` is `kras_mutation=1`, `nf1_loss=0`, and `erbb2_amplification=0`, with more negative dependency than all other lines.

judgments.text: The best-supported multi-feature subgroup for `dependency_RIT1` is `nf1_loss=1`, `lineage=brain`, and `cdkn2a_loss=0`, with more negative dependency than all other lines.

judgments.text: The best-supported multi-feature subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A` is `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, and `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, with more negative dependency than all other lines.
